docs.text: Replaces one parsed block. Arguments: newTags : replacement Tags object for the selected block. newText : optional replacement text for that block. i : 1-based block index.
docs.title: text\modifyBlock(newTags, newText = nil, i = 1)

docs.text: Moves first-category tags such as \pos , \move , \clip , and \fad into the first tag block. This mirrors how ASS effectively treats tags that should only have one active value per rendered line.
docs.title: text\moveToFirstLayer()

docs.text: Propagates missing previous tags into later blocks. Arguments: add_all : when true , propagates all missing tags. add_transforms : when true , also propagates \t(...) . This is especially useful before splitting a line into blocks or explicit line-break fragments.
docs.title: text\insertPendingTags(add_all = false, add_transforms = false)

docs.text: Presence checks across all parsed blocks.
docs.title: text\existsTag(name), text\existsTagAnd(...), and text\existsTagOr(...)

docs.text: Splits one raw line by explicit ASS line breaks while preserving the tags that remain in effect on each fragment. Arguments: text : raw dialogue string containing optional \N . Example: breaks, n = Text.getLineBreaks "{\\fs50}AB\\N{\\bord3}CD"
docs.title: Text.getLineBreaks(text)

docs.text: Line is the high-level helper that turns one subtitle line into something macros can split, reposition, expand frame by frame, or convert into geometry.
docs.title: Line

docs.text: Processes one line in place and fills cached style, metric, and positioning data. Arguments: ass : Ass context with styles and script metadata. line : dialogue line to enrich. This is the base step behind most text macros. It resolves the effective style, parses tags, computes metrics, and establishes the line's anchor position.
docs.title: Line.process(ass, line)

docs.text: Extends one processed line with line-break-level structures in line.lines . Arguments: ass : Ass context. line : source line. noblank : when true , blank fragments are filtered out. Example: Line.extend ass, l
docs.title: Line.extend(ass, line, noblank = true)

docs.text: Clears cached data and recomputes the extended structures. Arguments: ass : Ass context. line : line to refresh. noblank : forwarded to Line.extend .
docs.title: Line.update(ass, line, noblank = true)

docs.text: Splits an extended line into word fragments. Arguments: ass : Ass context. line : extended line. noblank : when true , blank-only fragments are skipped. Example: Line.extend ass, l words = Line.words ass, l, true
docs.title: Line.words(ass, line, noblank = false)

docs.text: Splits an extended line into UTF-8-aware character fragments. Arguments: ass : Ass context. line : extended line. noblank : when true , blank-only fragments are skipped.
docs.title: Line.chars(ass, line, noblank = false)

docs.text: Splits an extended line by \N . Arguments: ass : Ass context. line : extended line. noblank : when true , empty break fragments are skipped.
docs.title: Line.breaks(ass, line, noblank = false)

docs.text: Splits an extended line into one fragment per text/tag block. Arguments: ass : Ass context. line : extended line. noblank : when true , blank-only blocks are skipped.
docs.title: Line.tags(ass, line, noblank = false)

docs.text: Recomputes \pos or \move so a generated fragment keeps the same visual placement as the source line. Arguments: sourceLine : original unsplit line. generatedLine : derived fragment line. isMove : when true , returns a six-value \move payload instead of a two-value \pos . This is one of the core helpers behind Split Text .
docs.title: Line.reallocate(sourceLine, generatedLine, isMove = false)

docs.text: Calls fn once for each generated tag-block fragment after position tags are updated. Arguments: ass : Ass context. line : extended source line. fn : callback receiving (lineBlock, index) .
docs.title: Line.callBackTags(ass, line, fn)

docs.text: Calls fn once for each fragment after converting it to shape mode. Arguments: ass : Ass context. line : source line. fn : callback receiving (shapeLine, index) . This is the bridge between text workflows and geometry workflows.
docs.title: Line.callBackShape(ass, line, fn)

docs.text: Calls fn once for each fragment after expanding style transforms into final geometry. Arguments: ass : Ass context. line : source line. grid : optional {rows, cols, isBezier} descriptor used by envelope workflows. fn : callback receiving (expandedShapeLine, index) .
docs.title: Line.callBackExpand(ass, line, grid = nil, fn)

docs.text: Expands one line frame by frame using a step of 1 . Arguments: ass : Ass context. line : processed source line. fn : callback receiving (line, frame, endFrame, relativeIndex, totalFrames) .
docs.title: Line.callBackFBF(ass, line, fn)

docs.text: Expands one line into frame slices while materializing \move , \fade , and \t(...) . Arguments: ass : Ass context. line : processed source line. step : number of frames merged into each slice. fn : callback receiving (line, frame, endFrame, relativeIndex, totalFrames) . Example: Line.callBackFBFWithStep ass, l, 2, (line, frame, endFrame, i, n) -> ass\insertLine line, s
docs.title: Line.callBackFBFWithStep(ass, line, step = 1, fn)

docs.text: Changes alignment while compensating position so the rendered result stays visually stable. Arguments: line : source line. an : target ASS alignment. width : optional precomputed width. height : optional precomputed height.
docs.title: Line.changeAlign(line, an, width = nil, height = nil)

docs.text: Returns the effective xshad and yshad values after reconciling \shad , \xshad , and \yshad . Arguments: line : processed line.
docs.title: Line.solveShadow(line)

docs.text: Convert text into ASS drawing data or a Path . Arguments: line : line whose line.data and line.text_stripped should be converted. Example: shape = Line.toShape l path = Line.toPath l
docs.title: Line.toShape(line) and Line.toPath(line)
